intro: Croton-on-Hudson's tree-lined neighborhoods and historic homes provide ideal habitats for squirrels seeking shelter in attics, chimneys, and crawl spaces. faqs.question: How much does squirrel removal cost in Croton-on-Hudson?
faqs.answer: Squirrel removal services in Croton-on-Hudson typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the severity of the infestation, number of entry points, and whether attic cleanup or damage repair is needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: What are signs of squirrels in my Croton-on-Hudson attic?
faqs.answer: Common signs include scratching or scurrying sounds during dawn and dusk, visible droppings in the attic, chewed wood or wires, damaged insulation, and entry holes around soffits, vents, or roof edges. You may also notice squirrels entering and exiting your roofline during daylight hours.

faqs.question: Is squirrel removal humane in Croton-on-Hudson?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use humane live trapping and exclusion methods that comply with New York wildlife regulations. Squirrels are safely removed and relocated when appropriate, and we seal all entry points to prevent re-entry without harming the animals.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to squirrel problems in Croton-on-Hudson?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Croton-on-Hudson and Westchester County. Our team understands that squirrels can cause significant damage quickly, so we prioritize rapid response to assess the situation and begin removal as soon as possible.
